cases.title: CheckInTransaction atomically commits Present attendance and Consumed QrToken in single PostgreSQL transaction
preconditions: Real PostgreSQL test database running via Compose (not in-memory store)
Active session with enrolled student Pending attendance and Valid qr_tokens row
CheckInTransaction integration suite per testing-plan §5
CheckInService wired with AttendanceRecordRepository and QrTokenRepository
steps: Record beforeState: attendance status Pending, qr_token status Valid
Invoke CheckInService.submit with valid tokenId and in-radius locationPayload
Assert result outcome Success within single transaction boundary
Query attendance_records and assert status Present with non-null checkedInAt
Query qr_tokens and assert status Consumed with consumedByStudentId set
Verify both updates share same transaction commit timestamp (no intermediate visible state)
cases.expected: NFR-02 core invariant: successful check-in commits exactly one Present record and marks token Consumed atomically per 07-non-functional-risk §2.1 NFR-02

cases.title: DuplicateRace: 20 parallel CheckInService.submit calls for same student produce exactly one Present
preconditions: DuplicateRace integration suite per testing-plan §5
Real PostgreSQL with row-level lock on attendance_records
Active session, enrolled student with Pending attendance
20 parallel workers each holding valid tokenId
TestClock within attendance window and token TTL
steps: Launch 20 parallel CheckInService.submit invocations for identical studentId and sessionId
Await all completions and collect outcomes
Assert exactly 1 Success and 19 DuplicateCheckIn outcomes
Query attendance_records and assert single Present row with one checkedInAt timestamp
Query qr_tokens and assert exactly one token Consumed for the winning submission
cases.expected: 100% concurrent duplicate attempts produce correct final state: no double Present, no lost success per NFR-02 target and AC-09c

cases.title: ConcurrentCheckIn: 150 parallel unique-student submissions each commit one Present without data loss
preconditions: ConcurrentCheckIn integration suite per testing-plan §5
Active session with 150 enrolled students each with Pending attendance
150 unique Valid QrTokens (one per student)
Real PostgreSQL via Compose; API or CheckInService directly invocable
Each student has in-radius GPS fixture
steps: Launch 150 parallel check-in submissions staggered over 0–5 minutes (one per unique studentId)
Collect all outcomes and HTTP status codes
Assert 150 Success outcomes (zero lost successes)
Query attendance_records for sessionId and assert exactly 150 Present rows
Query qr_tokens and assert exactly 150 Consumed tokens matching successful submissions
Assert zero duplicate Present rows for any studentId
cases.expected: NFR-02 load target: 150 concurrent check-ins produce correct final state with 100% success rate and no double Present per verification method in 07-non-functional-risk §2.1

cases.title: TS-28: Parallel duplicate check-in requests from same student yield exactly one Present
preconditions: Real PostgreSQL test database with serializable or row-lock isolation
Active session, enrolled student with Pending attendance, Valid QrToken
Two parallel submission channels ready (HTTP or CheckInService.submit)
steps: Fire two parallel POST /check-in or CheckInService.submit calls for same studentId and sessionId
Collect both HTTP responses or CheckInResult outcomes
Assert exactly one response is HTTP 200 outcome Success with attendance Present
Assert the other response is HTTP 409 outcome DuplicateCheckIn
Query attendance_records and assert exactly one Present row for studentId and sessionId
Query check_in_attempts and assert one Success and one DuplicateCheckIn outcome
cases.expected: Testing-plan §3 TS-28 satisfied: concurrent duplicate requests produce exactly one Present; integrity preserved per NFR-02 and AC-09c

cases.title: Integration tests use real Compose PostgreSQL adapter; in-memory persistence fails NFR-02 ACID requirement
preconditions: Docker Compose db service running with postgres:15-alpine
DATABASE_URL=postgresql://wecheck:wecheck@localhost:5432/wecheck
Harness guardrail per docker-compose §5.2 forbids in-memory Map/SQLite as system of record
steps: Run integration test suite with Compose PostgreSQL connection
Execute CheckInTransaction test and assert Present + Consumed persisted after commit
Stop test DB connection and restart Compose db; re-run migration and seed
Verify attendance_records and qr_tokens tables receive durable writes (not process-local memory)
Confirm harness completion criteria reject in-memory-only repository adapters per §5.2
cases.expected: Real PostgreSQL required for NFR-02 ACID check-in transactions; in-memory stores cannot satisfy data integrity verification per 13-docker-compose-local-runtime §5.2

cases.title: POST /api/v1/check-in returns 200 Success with Present attendance when transaction commits atomically
preconditions: API running against Compose PostgreSQL
Authenticated enrolled Student with Pending attendance
Active session with Valid unexpired QrToken
In-radius coordinates (10.762700, 106.660200)
steps: Send POST /api/v1/check-in with body { tokenId, latitude, longitude, spoofMetadata: { mockLocationDetected: false } }
Assert HTTP status 200 OK
Assert response body outcome Success with attendance.status Present and non-null checkedInAt
Query qr_tokens for tokenId and assert status Consumed
Re-query attendance_records and confirm Present persisted (not transient response-only state)
cases.expected: HTTP 200 Success contract reflects committed transaction: Present record and Consumed token both durable per NFR-02 atomic commit requirement

cases.title: Check-in state transition: Pending attendance + Valid token → Present + Consumed in single atomic step
preconditions: Real PostgreSQL with attendance_records row beforeState Pending
qr_tokens row beforeState Valid linked to Active session
CheckInService.submit callable with in-radius coordinates
steps: Record beforeState: attendance Pending, qr_token Valid
Invoke CheckInService.submit for enrolled student
Assert afterState: attendance Present with checkedInAt set
Assert afterState: qr_token Consumed with consumedAt timestamp
Verify no intermediate state where Present exists but token remains Valid
Verify no intermediate state where token Consumed but attendance remains Pending
cases.expected: beforeState Pending+Valid; afterState Present+Consumed atomically; no partial commit visible per NFR-02 and validation-rules §5.1 transaction boundary

cases.title: Simulated transaction failure rolls back both attendance and token updates; no orphaned partial state
preconditions: Real PostgreSQL with injectable failure hook after attendance update but before token consume (or constraint violation fixture)
Active session with Pending attendance and Valid QrToken
CheckInService transaction wrapper per error-handling §1 data integrity
steps: Trigger CheckInService.submit with failure injection mid-transaction
Assert thrown error or failure outcome (not Success)
Query attendance_records and assert status remains Pending (rolled back)
Query qr_tokens and assert status remains Valid (not Consumed)
Verify check_in_attempts may log failure but no Present row exists
cases.expected: Transaction rollback on partial domain failure per NFR-02 and error-handling data integrity; no lost-success false negatives or partial commits

cases.title: Parallel same-token submissions from two students: exactly one Present, one TokenAlreadyUsed
preconditions: Real PostgreSQL with row-level lock on qr_tokens
Active session with two enrolled students Student A and Student B
Single Valid QrToken shared by parallel submission fixture
Both students have in-radius coordinates and authenticated sessions
steps: Launch two parallel CheckInService.submit calls: Student A and Student B with identical tokenId
Collect both outcomes
Assert exactly one Success with Present for winning student
Assert exactly one TokenAlreadyUsed for losing student
Query qr_tokens and assert single Consumed row (not double-consumed)
Query attendance_records and assert exactly one new Present row
cases.expected: BR-11 one-time-use token enforced under concurrency: atomic first-wins consumption; no double Present or token corruption per NFR-02

cases.title: Unique constraint on (session_id, student_id) prevents double Present at database boundary
preconditions: Real PostgreSQL with attendance_records unique index on (session_id, student_id) per domain model §4.2
Active session with student already Present
Attempt to insert second Present row bypassing application layer (direct SQL or race without lock)
steps: Confirm existing Present row for studentId and sessionId
Attempt direct INSERT of second attendance_records row with status Present for same pair
Assert PostgreSQL raises unique violation (23505) and transaction aborts
Re-query attendance_records and assert still exactly one Present row
Verify application-layer CheckInService returns DuplicateCheckIn before constraint if lock acquired first
cases.expected: Database-level integrity backstop for NFR-02: at most one Present per student per session even under application bypass attempts

cases.title: Flow A step 4: first successful check-in establishes atomic Present + Consumed integrity baseline
preconditions: Flow A steps 1–3 complete: roster seeded, session opened Active, QR token polled
Authenticated enrolled Student with Pending attendance
In-radius coordinates; tokenId from qrPayload in step 3
steps: POST /api/v1/check-in with tokenId from Flow A step 3 and in-radius coordinates
Assert HTTP 200 outcome Success with attendance Present
Query qr_tokens and assert token status Consumed
Query attendance_records and assert exactly one Present row for studentId and sessionId
Instructor GET /api/v1/sessions/:sessionId/attendance shows Present count incremented by 1
cases.expected: Flow A happy-path check-in satisfies NFR-02 baseline: one Present record and one-time token consumption committed atomically

cases.title: Flow B anti-fraud path preserves transaction invariants after duplicate and token-reuse rejections
preconditions: Flow B steps 1–2 complete: Active session, Student A checked in successfully
Student A Present; token from step 2 Consumed
Student B enrolled with Pending attendance
steps: Student A POST /api/v1/check-in with fresh Valid token and assert HTTP 409 DuplicateCheckIn
Verify Student A still has exactly one Present row unchanged
Student B POST /api/v1/check-in with same Consumed tokenId and assert HTTP 400 TokenAlreadyUsed
Verify Student B attendance remains Pending (no partial Present)
Verify security log entry for Student B without corrupting Student A Present state
cases.expected: Flow B rejection paths maintain NFR-02 invariants: failed attempts do not corrupt committed Present/Consumed state or create duplicate records

cases.title: POST /api/v1/check-in returns 409 DuplicateCheckIn without consuming token or altering Present timestamp
preconditions: Student already Present for session with checkedInAt T0
Fresh Valid QrToken T1 not yet consumed
Authenticated Student session
steps: POST /api/v1/check-in with tokenId T1 and in-radius coordinates
Assert HTTP status 409 Conflict with outcome DuplicateCheckIn
Query qr_tokens for T1 and assert status remains Valid
Query attendance_records and assert checkedInAt unchanged at T0
Assert response envelope contains no attendance.status Present update on failure
cases.expected: DuplicateCheckIn HTTP contract preserves NFR-02 integrity: rejection does not partially consume token or mutate committed Present record

cases.title: 150 staggered POST /api/v1/check-in requests return 200 Success with zero 5xx data-corruption responses
preconditions: Full E2E stack: PostgreSQL, API on port 3001
Active session with 150 enrolled students and unique Valid tokens each
Load generator or parallel test harness with staggered 0–5 minute window
steps: Launch 150 concurrent POST /api/v1/check-in requests staggered over 0–5 minutes (one per unique student) per testing-plan §7 Peak concurrent check-in
Collect all HTTP status codes and response outcomes
Assert 150 responses with HTTP 200 outcome Success
Assert zero HTTP 500, 502, or 503 responses indicating transaction corruption
Assert overall error rate < 0.1% per testing-plan §7 pass criteria
Final DB query: exactly 150 Present rows, 150 Consumed tokens, zero duplicates
cases.expected: NFR-02 under pilot-scale load: 100% of valid concurrent check-ins produce correct committed final state with no lost successes and error rate < 0.1% per 07-non-functional-risk §2.1 and testing-plan §7

cases.title: Student mobile check-in shows success confirmation only after server commits Present transaction
preconditions: Full-preview or dev stack with web on port 3000 and API + PostgreSQL healthy
Authenticated Student on mobile viewport (375×812)
Active session with valid check-in deep link and in-radius GPS mock
Student attendance beforeState Pending
steps: Navigate to /check-in deep link with valid tokenId and sessionId
Assert GET /api/v1/check-in/tokens/:tokenId/preflight returns HTTP 200 and GPS capture step mounts with session summary per AC-07c
Complete GPS capture and tap submit control
Wait for server response (no optimistic success before network completes)
Assert Vietnamese success confirmation appears with Present status
Navigate to /history and assert session shows Present with check-in timestamp
Refresh page and verify Present status persists (committed to DB, not client cache only)
cases.expected: UI reflects NFR-02 server-authoritative state: success confirmation and history badge appear only after atomic transaction commit; refresh confirms durable Present record per AC-07a

cases.title: Duplicate mobile check-in shows DuplicateCheckIn UI without creating second Present in history
preconditions: Authenticated Student on mobile viewport already Present for session
Active session with valid check-in deep link
GPS permission granted with in-radius coordinates
steps: Navigate to /check-in deep link for session where student is already Present
Complete preflight and GPS capture steps with in-radius coordinates
Tap submit control and await server response
Verify CheckInOutcomePanel displays DuplicateCheckIn with message Bạn đã điểm danh buổi học này rồi
Navigate to /history and assert exactly one Present entry for the session (no duplicate rows)
Verify prior check-in timestamp unchanged from first successful submission
cases.expected: NFR-02 integrity visible in UI: duplicate attempt rejected client-side after server 409; history confirms single committed Present record per student per session

cases.title: TokenAlreadyUsed rejection leaves qr_token Consumed state unchanged and loser attendance Pending
preconditions: QrToken already Consumed by Student A
Student B enrolled with Pending attendance
Token within 30-second validity window (not ExpiredQr path)
steps: Invoke CheckInService.submit for Student B with Consumed tokenId
Assert outcome TokenAlreadyUsed (not Success)
Query qr_tokens and assert status remains Consumed (no re-transition or corruption)
Query attendance_records for Student B and assert status Pending
Verify security audit entry created without altering Student A Present record
cases.expected: NFR-02 failure path preserves committed state: TokenAlreadyUsed does not roll back winner transaction or create partial Present for loser

cases.title: CheckInTransaction consumes QrToken only on Success; TokenAlreadyUsed path rolls back without Present update
preconditions: Real PostgreSQL with qr_tokens row status Consumed
Enrolled Student B with Pending attendance
CheckInTransaction suite per testing-plan §5
steps: Invoke CheckInService.submit with Consumed tokenId for Student B
Assert outcome TokenAlreadyUsed and no Present update for Student B
Assert qr_tokens row remains Consumed (no double-consumption attempt persisted)
Contrast: successful submit on Valid token atomically sets Consumed and Present in single commit
Verify check_in_attempts row logged with outcome TokenAlreadyUsed
cases.expected: CheckInTransaction NFR-02 target: Present + token consumed atomically on Success only; reuse rejection does not corrupt attendance or token state
